The exhibit is making its Southeastern debut at the Upcountry History Museum in June. It features archival [...]

items.title: Keir Starmer announces he'll resign as UK prime minister, kicking off contest for Labour successor
items.description: LONDON (AP) — British Prime Minister Keir Starmer said Monday he is stepping down as leader of the governing Labour Party and will leave office within weeks, forced out by his own party scarcely two years after being elected in a landslide. Starmer says he will remain caretaker prime minister until the party chooses a new Labour [...]

items.title: Storm Chances Increase Monday; 90-Degree Heat Returns by Weekend
items.description: (WSPA) -- Seasonably warm and humid today with highs in the upper 80s and mid-80s in the mountains. We're mostly dry into mid-week and back to summertime pop-up [...]

items.title: I just wanted to say
items.description: I just wanted to say thank you to everyone who continues to show up for us and those who are new to our cuisine!🙏🏾❤️ I appreciate you spending your hard earned money with us and coming back for more and sending your friends and family. I’m beginning to know your faces and sometimes your orders (unless you’re trying something new)! I’m grateful beyond measure🙏🏾🙏🏾🙏🏾 thank you 🫶🏽🫶🏽🫶🏽.

items.title: I don’t like complaining but
items.description:
I don’t like complaining but i had to in this case. I had my Father’s Day meal with my wife, daughter and son-in-law at Venus Pie. When i say there wasn’t much evidence of toppings, i’m not exaggerating. i paid $2 per topping for beef, onions and black olives. i could see the olives but the other two; let’s just say a smidgen may have been seen. In addition, my daughter and son-in-law had a beef calzone with onions. i have never seen so much cheese with very little evidence of beef. 
I loved the

items.description: The Juneteenth Block Party is TODAY! items.title: This week we got together as a group named "PedalBurg" to bike around the city of Spartanburg! Biking in larger numbers ...
items.description:
This week we got together as a group named "PedalBurg" to bike around the city of Spartanburg! 
Biking in larger numbers is safer and it helps send a message to the city: we want to bike!

With biking infrastructure steadily growing around the Burg, people are able to utilize this method of transportation more often and more safely. We'd love to see even more bike friendly roads continue to be adopted 🚲

#strongtowns #strongtownsspartanburg #pedalburg #biking

Whether you’re here for work, a concert, a weekend getaway, or to explore everything Spartanburg has to offer, Fairfield Inn is ready to welcome you with comfortable accommodations and top notch service. items.title: One of our favorite summer traditions! ☀️ Last Friday, we gathered for our annual company beach day. From lunch at The @...
items.description:
One of our favorite summer traditions! ☀️
 
Last Friday, we gathered for our annual company beach day. From lunch at The @windjammeriop to an afternoon on the beach, it was the perfect way to kick off the weekend.
 
A few out-of-town folks stayed a little longer, enjoying some extra beach time and a Mt. Joy concert before heading home. 🎶🏖️

#swpeople #swculture #beachday

items.description: Join Mayor Jerome Rice for a special Juneteenth edition of Miles with the Mayor this Saturday, June 20th at 8 AM! Lace up your walking shoes and join the youth from our various community programs as we take steps toward remembering our past and building our future.

items.title: The Land, the Home, and What Lasts We think about legacy a lot in this work. Not just the legacy of a beautiful home, bu...
items.description: The Land, the Home, and What Lasts We think about legacy a lot in this work. Not just the legacy of a beautiful home, but the legacy of how it sits on its land. Whether it depletes the resources around it or replenishes them. Whether it creates a connection to place that deepens over time. Native landscaping eliminates most irrigation demand and restores habitat for the wildlife that belongs there. Local materials reduce the energy cost of transportation and invest in regional craftspeople. Smar

items.title: Six years ago this week, on June 20, 2020, the day after Juneteenth, sixteen artists spent a single day painting “Black...
items.description: Six years ago this week, on June 20, 2020, the day after Juneteenth, sixteen artists spent a single day painting “Black Lives Matter” on W Broad St. in front of Spartanburg City Hall.  It became the first mural of its kind in South Carolina, but as those who created it will tell you, the paint was only part of the story. The day itself was just as important, bringing our community together and providing deep local meaning for the people of Spartanburg. W Broad St. is changing now, and the mural'

items.description: As temperatures rise, please remember: Do NOT leave your pets unattended in a vehicle. Even with the windows cracked, temperatures inside a vehicle can quickly reach dangerous levels and put your pet’s life at risk. If your pet cannot go inside with you, please leave them safely at home. A quick errand is not worth risking your pet’s life. ☀️ SUMMER HEAT CAN TURN DEADLY IN MINUTES ☀️ As temperatures rise, please remember: Do NOT leave your pets unattended in a vehicle. Great conversations are even better over lunch.

Recently, we hosted a Lunch & Learn with @knights_companies and @seamonwhiteside at SW’s office, where we brought Chipotle and spent time discussing precast solutions, industry practices, and collaboration. These meetings provide a valuable opportunity for contractors, engineers, and suppliers to connect, share insights, and stay aligned on both current and future projects.

items.title: Taking grading beyond the screen. 🚧 Our first Grading Workshop brought team members from our Charlotte, Greenville, and...
items.description:
Taking grading beyond the screen. 🚧
 
Our first Grading Workshop brought team members from our Charlotte, Greenville, and Mount Pleasant offices together to connect design intent to real-world field conditions through 3D modeling exercises and hands-on demonstrations. Team members even took the lesson outside, using string lines to model grading concepts in the field and better understand how design decisions shape site conditions.
 
A great two days of collaboration, problem-solving, and knowle

items.title: The Palmetto Stop Is State Law in SC
items.description: On May 18th, 2026, South Carolina Governor Henry McMaster (R) signed into law S.812 , or the Stop-as-Yield Law for Bicycles. This makes South Carolina the 14th U.S. state to pass a version of the “Idaho Stop”, with Palmetto Walk Bike dubbing ours the “Palmetto Stop.” This is an exciting development for cycling safety in our state and enjoyed bipartisan support. The bill was introduced by Senator Luke A. Rankin (R-Horry), cosponsored by Senator Overture Walker (D-Richland), and supported in commi

items.title: Here’s something we tell every client: a home that performs well for the environment will perform well for you, too. Bet...
items.description: Here’s something we tell every client: a home that performs well for the environment will perform well for you, too. Better indoor air quality. More natural daylight. Passive cooling and heating that reduces your energy bill and your reliance on mechanical systems. Materials that don’t off-gas harmful compounds into the air your family breathes. These aren’t luxury features. They’re good design. We designed South Carolina’s first LEED Platinum building, and we’ve been applying that same integrat

items.description: As Spartanburg County continues to grow, local school districts have worked to expand capacity, modernize learning environments, and prepare students for the future. Since 2016, all seven Spartanburg County school districts have passed referendums supporting more than $1.3 billion in school facility investments. items.description: Save the date for Miles with the Mayor happening next Saturday, June 20th starting at 8 AM at the Southside Cultural Monument (corner of S Converse St. & Hudson Barksdale Blvd.). All are invited to join Mayor Jerome Rice for this special Juneteenth walk, celebrating Black community leaders and the rich cultural history reflected in the Southside Cultural Monument! This month's walk is sponsored by The Landie Scholarship Fund & Bruh Mentor.

items.title: A beautiful evening spent slowing down, creating, and connecting 🌸💐 Our Charlotte Women’s Initiative recently enjoyed ...
items.description:
A beautiful evening spent slowing down, creating, and connecting 🌸💐
 
Our Charlotte Women’s Initiative recently enjoyed a Garden Gathering, where we created our own bouquets and enjoyed a guided meditation. It was the perfect reminder to pause, be present, and enjoy time together.
 
Thank you to @alfredwilliams_carolinas for hosting us, and to Jess from @gatherflowerstudio for sharing her creativity and helping make the evening so special.

#SWculture #SWpeople #womensinitiative

Register at https://runsignup.com/…/…/Spartanburg/RunForFathers5KWalkRun. It’s that time again for our annual Spartanburg Run for Fathers 5K! items.title: This year's Red, White & Boom kicks off with a special pre-show featuring Shot Thru the Heart - The Bon Jovi Experience...
items.description: This year's Red, White & Boom kicks off with a special pre-show featuring Shot Thru the Heart - The Bon Jovi Experience taking place on Morgan Square from 2 PM-5 PM! Following the FREE pre-show, join us in a fun, patriotic parade walk to Fifth Third Park where the festivities continue from 5 PM until the big BOOM--a professional fireworks show to honor America's 250th anniversary! 🎆 We can't wait to celebrate the USA with you all on July 4th with free, family-friendly fun in Downtown Spartanburg

items.title: A building doesn’t appear overnight. It starts with a vision, a piece of property, and a whole lot of work that most peo...
items.description:
A building doesn’t appear overnight.

It starts with a vision, a piece of property, and a whole lot of work that most people never see.

Last week, we reached another important milestone at @christchurchanglicanmtp with the completion of the slab.

The groundwork has been laid. The foundation is in place. And soon, what has existed on paper for months will begin rising above the site for everyone to see.

These are the moments we love in construction. Watching an idea become something real.

items.title: Happy National Trails Day! 🌳 Trails shape the way people move through and experience a community, creating space for co...
items.description:
Happy National Trails Day! 🌳

Trails shape the way people move through and experience a community, creating space for connection, exploration, and everyday access to the outdoors. We’re grateful to help support spaces like these and the role they play in our communities.

What do you enjoy most when you’re on a trail? 🥾
 
#NationalTrailsDay #SWprojects #Trails #CommunityDesign

items.title: OneSpartanburg, Inc. is not affiliated with any group or funding effort attempting to influence County Council elections...
items.description: OneSpartanburg, Inc. is not affiliated with any group or funding effort attempting to influence County Council elections. Claims by candidates that we are linked to spending behind recent mailers, and that we invited candidates to meet with our investors, are false. We do aim to educate our constituents and partners on local candidates’ views on economic issues. We invited candidates to speak to municipal leaders at our regularly scheduled Mayoral meeting, and invited candidates to respond to a

items.title: Johnston Design Group Posted
One year and ten days ago, Johnston Design Group and Edifice were hired to design and build a new stadium for Triumph SC...
items.description: One year and ten days ago, Johnston Design Group and Edifice were hired to design and build a new stadium for Triumph SC. Three weeks later, graders were already on site. And less than a year after that — we were there for the first game, the first roar of the crowd, and the first victory in their new home. It was a tall order, and it took an extraordinary team to deliver it — the City of Greenville, the Triumph organization, our partners at Edifice, and every person on the design and constructi

items.description: I think Spring is both ways because they added that unnecessary right turn lane going in.  I think Broad is enter only and Kennedy will serve as the main enter/exit.

items.title: Early voting is winding down ahead of the June 9 Primary, with contested races for both Spartanburg County Council and S...
items.description: Early voting is winding down ahead of the June 9 Primary, with contested races for both Spartanburg County Council and SC House seats. OneSpartanburg, Inc. has sought to educate voters and our county’s business community on where candidates stand on important issues, as chambers of commerce across the country have done for hundreds of years. To learn more about where the candidates on June’s Primary ballot stand, head to https://www.onespartanburginc.com/vote.

items.title: New City/County Government Center
items.description: Steel is up to (essentially) roof level now!  We can begin to get a sense for the height/presence of the building.  Also, steel is finally going up on the north end of the building.  The parking deck has reached the central stair/elevator tower that will have pedestrian access to the interior plaza, and you can see the center car entrance on Spring too.

Every day, most of us reserve a little time for ourselves. A quiet cup of coffee. A walk. A moment to breathe. What if y...
items.description: Every day, most of us reserve a little time for ourselves. A quiet cup of coffee. A walk. A moment to breathe. What if your home did that for you — all the time? That's the idea behind Living Wellness in restorative design. Not wellness as a checklist, but as something built into the way a home is made: the quality of its light, the freshness of its air, its relationship to the land it sits on, the peace that comes from a space designed with you — your life, your rhythms, your wellbeing — in min

Rain? ✅ Mud? ✅ 24 straight hours of teamwork? ✅

items.title: The Spartanburg area gained 400 jobs in March 2026 and has averaged approximately 255 new jobs per month over the past f...
items.description: The Spartanburg area gained 400 jobs in March 2026 and has averaged approximately 255 new jobs per month over the past five years, continuing a broader trend of workforce and economic growth across the region. Job growth impacts more than just businesses. It affects career opportunities, household stability, workforce development and long-term economic momentum across Spartanburg County. Growth conversations are also conversations about jobs, opportunity and the future of our community. Source:

items.description: “We are thrilled to have this opportunity to make specialty services more available to our community, as well as the other outpatient services that will now be available under one roof in this beautiful new building; and we are so grateful to SC DHHS for the funds that helped make this project possible,” said Dr. Matthew Logan, President & CEO of Self Regional Healthcare in a release. items.title: Will our civil designer stick the backflip? 🕺✨ From the office to the dance floor, Brian Titze absolutely crushed it at...
items.description:
Will our civil designer stick the backflip? 🕺✨
 
From the office to the dance floor, Brian Titze absolutely crushed it at Dancing With The ARK’s Stars, taking home the Judges Choice Award after weeks of rehearsals and fundraising $20,100 for an amazing cause. Safe to say he’s got moves 👏

@thearkofsc 

#swpeople #dancingwiththearkstars

items.title: The flower baskets are officially back downtown! 💐🌸  You’ll spot them hanging from lamp posts all around downtown Spar...
items.description: The flower baskets are officially back downtown! 💐🌸 You’ll spot them hanging from lamp posts all around downtown Spartanburg, adding a little extra color to your walk, your coffee run, your dinner plans, or wherever the day takes you. Come out to see them and make an afternoon out of it: grab a bite at your favorite local spot, pop into a few shops, take the long way around, and enjoy downtown while everything’s in bloom. There’s a lot to see right now ✨ #downtownspartanburg #spartanburgsc #ther

items.title: Strong Towns Spartanburg is hosting a group bike ride called Pedal Burg 🌼! Join us at Fretwell on Wednesday, June 17th ...
items.description:
Strong Towns Spartanburg is hosting a group bike ride called Pedal Burg 🌼! Join us at Fretwell on Wednesday, June 17th at 6:30 pm

We'll start the ride at Fretwell, go through the route twice, and then end back at Fretwell. The ride takes roughly 15-20 minutes and we will be going at a leasurely pace. 
Children are more than welcome to join!

The reasons we are hosting a group bike ride:
1. There is safety in numbers. Having a bunch of people biking at the same time is generally safer around car

items.title: Twenty-five years. Same mission. When JDG opened its doors in Greenville in 2001, we believed architecture could do more...
items.description: Twenty-five years. Same mission. When JDG opened its doors in Greenville in 2001, we believed architecture could do more than shelter — it could restore. Restore well-being. Restore community. Restore our relationship with the places we inhabit. That belief has never wavered. And as we step into our next chapter, we're returning to it with fresh energy and deep gratitude for the Upstate community that has grown alongside us. Here's to the next 25 — together. #JDG #RestorativeDesign #Greenville #

items.title: Three Ways You Can Make Your City a Better Place
items.description: Last month was a super busy month! I have been focusing on The Walking Spartan Instagram while I figure out what else I want to do over here on the Substack. Last month’s back-to-back East Main Road Diet coverage and Amtrak trip to Greenville was so fun but also some of the most energy I have invested into this platform to date. I am immensely grateful for the work that I get to do and all of the great connections I have made through this journey. I figure now is a perfect time to slow down and
